name: Sugar
summary: Sugar is a sweetener used for taste, texture, and preservation.
function: It adds sweetness and can help balance acidity or support shelf life.

name: Brown Cane Sugar
summary: Brown cane sugar is a less-refined sugar used for sweetness and flavor.
function: It sweetens the product and can add a deeper caramel-like taste.

name: Salt
summary: Salt is a mineral seasoning used for flavor and preservation.
function: It boosts flavor and can help preserve packaged foods.

name: Citric Acid
summary: Citric acid is commonly used to adjust acidity and stabilize flavor.
function: It helps control tartness and can support freshness in packaged foods.

name: Soy Lecithin
summary: Soy lecithin is an emulsifier that helps oil and water stay mixed.
function: It improves texture and consistency in chocolate, sauces, and baked goods.

name: Whey
summary: Whey is a milk-derived ingredient used for protein, texture, or flavor.
function: It can add dairy solids, body, or protein to packaged foods.

name: Natural Flavor
summary: Natural flavor is a label term for flavoring ingredients derived from natural sources.
function: It is used to shape or reinforce taste, but labels rarely list the full flavor blend.

name: Modified Starch
summary: Modified starch is processed to change how it thickens or stabilizes food.
function: It can improve texture, prevent separation, or help sauces stay smooth.

name: Radish
summary: Radish is a root vegetable commonly eaten raw for its crisp texture and peppery taste.
function: It is a whole-food ingredient rather than an additive or processing aid.

name: Fig
summary: Fig is a fruit used for sweetness, texture, and fruit flavor.
function: It is a whole-fruit ingredient rather than an additive.

name: Black Pepper
summary: Black pepper is a spice made from dried peppercorns and used for flavor.
function: It is a straightforward seasoning ingredient, not a food additive.

name: Concentrated Lemon Juice
summary: Concentrated lemon juice is used for acidity and brightness.
function: It can balance sweetness and help preserve flavor in fruit products.

name: Fruit Pectin
summary: Fruit pectin is a gelling fiber commonly used in jams and fruit preserves.
function: It helps fruit mixtures set into a spreadable texture.

name: Aqua
summary: Aqua is water, often used as the main solvent in personal care products.
function: It dissolves or carries other ingredients in lotions, cleansers, and serums.

name: Glycerin
summary: Glycerin is a humectant that helps draw water to the skin or hair.
function: It is commonly used to support moisture retention in beauty formulas.

name: Parfum
summary: Parfum is a label term for fragrance blends used to scent a product.
function: It adds scent, but labels usually do not list each fragrance component individually.

name: Phenoxyethanol
summary: Phenoxyethanol is a preservative used to help keep formulas stable.
function: It helps reduce microbial growth in lotions, cleansers, and makeup.

name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
summary: Sodium laureth sulfate is a cleansing surfactant used in wash-off products.
function: It helps water lift oils and dirt away from skin or hair.

name: Alcohol Denat.
summary: Denatured alcohol is used as a fast-evaporating solvent in some beauty products.
function: It can help formulas dry quickly or feel lighter on the skin.

name: Tocopherol
summary: Tocopherol is a form of vitamin E used as an antioxidant in formulas.
function: It can help support stability by slowing oxidation in oils and emulsions.
